violations.text: Establishment must register an individual with the Rhode Island Food Safety Manager license. Application provided during time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2022
The following were observed in poor repair: 1. gaskets to 1 door lowboy freezer 2. glass door to retail reach-in cooler New Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the sides of 1 door lowboy freezer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2025
Coving is missing and/or in poor repair in beverage storage room and walk-in cooler. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Application provided during time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2022

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Work order in place. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2025
The following were observed in poor repair: 1. gaskets to 1 door lowboy freezer 2. glass door to retail reach-in cooler Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: 1. sides of 1 door lowboy freezer 2. single service item rack 3. fanguards in walk-in cooler 4. under drink dispensing units Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2025
Coving is missing and/or in poor repair in beverage storage room and walk-in cooler. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2025

No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Application provided during time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2022

1. Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. 2. Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink near the beverage station. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. 3. The hand-washing sink by the beverage station was lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
Rodent dropping observed in direct contact with box of dairy and gallon of juice. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Food contact surfaces of food roller were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Hamburgers and Sandwiches were observed held at 116 and 128 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed during time of inspection and staff voluntarily disposed of foods due to quality when foods are reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Cheeses, Bologna, and Milk were observed held at between 45-48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods out of temperature for an unknown amount of time shall be voluntarily disposed of. Ambient temperature of walk-in cooler varied between 43-45 degrees Fahrenheit. All TCS foods moved to other refrigeration during time of inspection. Equipment to be properly adjusted and in good repair prior to storing TCS foods. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Rodenticide was observed placed in food prep area and in office area without containment. Rodent bait shall be contained in a covered, tamper-resistant bait station. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

The presence of small rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings observed on box of food in walk-in cooler, rodent droppings observed on floor in interior perimeter of walk-in cooler and perimeter of beverage room leading to walk-in cooler, as well as rodent dropping observed in direct contact with single use parchment paper for food. Droppings also observed in back utility room. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment to work with commercial pest control company to eliminate activity and/or bring pest activity under control. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2025

Food and beverages were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler, walk-in freezer, storage areas and retail store space. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
The following were observed in poor repair:1. plastic curtain for food rollers2. gaskets to 1 door lowboy freezer3. glass door to retail reach-in cooler New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:1. sides of 1 door lowboy freezer2. single service item rack3. fanguards in walk-in cooler4. ice accumulation and black residue in interior of chest icecream freezer5. under drink dispensing units New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2025
Refuse area was observed with dried leaves and litter. Area to be maintained to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment, clutter and items were observed by associate area and back storage room. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2025

The floors under equipment in back room has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2025

Some areas of walk-in cooler floor were observed lined with cardboard. Cardboard was observed with debris, gnaw marks, and rodent droppings. Establishment to cease practice. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
Coving is missing and/or in poor repair in beverage storage room and walk-in cooler. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2025
